question: What is the difference between a silent antagonist and an inverse agonist?
options: Silent antagonists reduce basal activity, inverse agonists do not
Silent antagonists have no effect on basal activity, inverse agonists reduce it
Silent agonists are always more potent
explanation: Silent antagonists block agonist binding without affecting basal receptor activity. Inverse agonists actively reduce constitutive receptor activity below basal levels.
